kit_price.text: **$49** (one-time, lifetime updates to the v0.x line).

pack_pwyw.text: **Pay-what-you-want, $19 suggested** (one-time, free updates to the v0.x line).

bundle_price_block.text: **$59** for the bundle — versus **$68** bought separately ($49 kit + $19 suggested for the pack). The $9 discount is a modest, honest nudge

bundle_faq.text: Yes — each is sold separately (kit $49, pack $19 PWYW). The bundle is only for people who want both; it saves $9 and one checkout.

click_row_floor_open.text: price set ($19 pay-what-you-want suggested (default); minimum owner's choice)

sibling_committed_floor.text: Price: $5 fixed per pack (default — §3 evidence; floor $3; ratified by ORDER 010 / V039 …)

coherence_guard.text: The bundle price is FIXED even though the pack alone is PWYW — inheriting PWYW would let the bundle undercut the $49 kit alone, which is incoherent pricing.

v040_ratification.text: $59 one-time fixed RATIFIED — ORDER 010 / V040 … **$64 is PARKED-WITH-BAR** behind one named retention measurement — ≥ 50589/54944 ≈ 0.9207. **Never price at $68**

fee_schedule.text: flat **10% platform fee + $0.50 per transaction** … payment processing (~2.9% + $0.30) … roughly **~12.9% + $0.80 per sale**

hard_gate.text: HARD-GATED: blocked until the ⚑B and ⚑D component publish clicks are executed.

_grounding.harvest_anomalies: ZERO — every pinned constant and quoted line matched verbatim at venture-lab 520bdfc and in the in-tree V040 artifacts

floor_grid_notes.0: decision cell — the floorless default of the queued ⚑D click's 'minimum owner's choice'
floor_grid_notes.3: the sibling photo-packs family's ONE committed floor (OWNER-QUEUE.md:84)

fee_affine.net_formula: net(P) = (871/1000)·P − 4/5 per transaction (V040's committed reading of MARKET-PLAN.md:17-19)

model.buyer: a both-buyer with two purchase paths: BUNDLE = one checkout, pay p; SEPARATE = two checkouts, pay a + max(w, f) where w is the intended pack payment; STRATEGIC buyer pays w = f; types exogenous — no demand size (V040 G1 territory), no conversion

quantities.sigma_adv: (a + s) − p (advertised saving, the copy's basis)
quantities.wstar: p − a (buyer-type indifference; bundle weakly cheaper iff w ≥ w*, ties to bundle)
quantities.coherence: p lattice-coherent iff a ≤ p ≤ a + f (never undercuts the kit alone; never charges a strategic premium)
quantities.fstar: f*(p) = p − a (floor threshold; = w* identity, an F1 gate)
quantities.seller_delta: Δ(f) = net(p) − [net(a) + net(f)] = α·(p − a − f) + β (bundle-minus-strategic-separate seller net)

theorems.T1_basis_gap: σ_adv − σ_ach(f) = s − f EXACTLY, for every a and every p (both cancel); at f = 0 the '$9 saving' overstates the achievable by $19 = s exactly — the achievable saving is −$10
theorems.T2_coherence_window: coherent iff p ∈ [a, a + f]; f*(p) = p − a unit slope; {59, 64, 68} → f* = {10, 15, 19}; f*(68) = 19 = s EXACTLY (registered margin-0 contact); f*(59) = 10 > 19/2 (ratio 20/19); at f = 0 the window is the single point {49}; strict achievable discount $d requires f ≥ (p − a) + d, so the committed '$9' needs f = 19 = s
theorems.T3_fee_invariant: under ANY affine net(P) = αP − β (α ∈ (0,1), β ≥ 0): bundle-minus-strategic-separate seller net = α·(p − a − f) + β; at the decision cell the seller nets exactly +$9.51 while the buyer pays $10 over the achievable path; simultaneous-strict for every f < 10; the ONLY f-independent genuine saving is β = $0.80, the saved second fixed fee, accruing to the SELLER; Δ(19) = −7039/1000 reproduces V040's committed nudge cost

external_anchors_F1.nudge_cost: Δ(19) = −7039/1000 = −$7.039 (V040's committed nudge cost, external anchor)

hand_world_F4.pencil: separate at floor = 3, bundle = 4 → π = 1; advertised basis 3+2 = 5 → σ_adv = 1; achievable 3+0 = 3 → σ_ach = −1; gap = 2 = s; only q = 3 satisfies 3 ≤ q ≤ 3

degeneracies_F5.f_eq_s: f = 19 = s → PWYW degenerate-fixed: gap 0, triple coherent, σ_ach = σ_adv = 9 (the world the committed copy implicitly assumes)
degeneracies_F5.p_eq_a: p = 49 = a → pack-free-with-kit: coherent at EVERY f with π = 0 (the guard sentence as arithmetic — the only coherent price at f = 0)
degeneracies_F5.p_eq_a_plus_s: p = 68 = a + s → f* = 19 = s (the banned-anchor contact)

t1_independence_controls.law: gap(a, p, f) = s − f at EVERY (a, p, f) cell — any world where the gap moves with a or p kills T1

t3_affine_family_check.law: Δ computed as net(p) − [net(a) + net(f)] must equal α·(p − a − f) + β at every (α, β) × grid cell; decision clauses never ride the fee layer

arm_R.role: REPORTING-ONLY — buyer-type draws at the decision cell; NO statistical gate rides Arm R
arm_R.draw_discipline: ONE uniform per episode (random.Random(seed).random()), mapped to w by cumulative pmf; path choice = min cost with ties to bundle (w ≥ w*); draw counts counted and asserted
arm_R.disclosed_reporting_landing: NONE — the registration discloses exact expectations only, no seeded counts; the empirical counts print beside the exact expectations and are not compared to any disclosed value

seeds.provenance: the drafter's registered allocation IS the session seed set (V077–V086 precedent); 20261650–653 are P073/V086's registered set; the gap 20261654–659 is the drafter's disclosed in-flight buffer, unused; sweep of this clone at origin/main 9e22abb maxes at genuine seed 20261653 — the lone in-tree hit on the digit-string 20261664 is a DATA numeral (0.00202616643 in verdict-032 results.json), not a seed

REJECT.R1_incoherence_at_default: at the decision cell p ∉ [a, a + f] (the lane's own no-undercut guard formalized excludes the committed price) AND σ_ach(0) ≤ −(p − a) with σ_adv = 9 exactly reproducing the committed copy number (the same triple carries a +$9 advertised and a −$10 achievable saving simultaneously)
REJECT.R2_anchor_family_lattice: f*({59, 64, 68}) = {10, 15, 19} with f*(68) = s EXACTLY (margin-0, registered) and f*(59) > s/2 strictly (ratio 20/19)
REJECT.R3_repair_thresholds: f = 10 restores weak coherence with σ_ach = 0 exactly; strict achievable saving $d requires f ≥ 10 + d; the committed '$9' on the achievable basis requires f = 19 = s

decision_rule.APPROVE: at the decision cell p ∈ [a, a + f] AND σ_ach ≥ 0 — mutually exclusive with R1 by arithmetic; fires on the f grid at every cell f ≥ 10 (liveness witnesses checked)
NULL_axes: basis-reading fork (vs-suggested reading TRUE as stated — ship the exact gap table s − f, repair = one clarifying phrase)
floor-granularity convention (integer thresholds pre-checked non-flipping under sub-dollar quanta)
theorem failure without gate failure (corrected law is the finding)

must_equal_rows_registered_contacts: σ_ach(0) = −(p − a) = −10 (R1 at equality by construction)
f*(68) = 19 = s (the registered margin-0 contact — the thesis: V040's banned anchor coheres only when PWYW is dead ink)
σ_ach(10) = 0 (the repair threshold at exact weak coherence)
f(d = 9) = 19 = s (the committed '$9' needs PWYW extinct)
gap(f) = s − f at every grid cell (T1 as equality)
the five V040 external anchors (nets, +4/5 row, retention bar, Δ(19) = −7039/1000)
must_clear_rows_strict: incoherence margins at f ∈ {0, 1, 3, 5}: p − (a + f) = 10, 9, 7, 5 all ≥ 1
coherence margins at f ∈ {15, 19}: (a + f) − p = 5, 9 all ≥ 1 (f = 10 is the registered boundary cell, must-equal)
margin_ledger.policy: the V086 typed-ledger convention: must-equal rows (registered contacts) are gated as two-sided EQUALITIES — drift in either direction falsifies the boundary-location claim; must-clear rows gate margin > 0; gate = no UNregistered decision comparison sits at margin 0. Robustness note (reporting-only, never a decision code path): the R1 incoherence-at-default clause is arithmetically independent of the $68/f = s margin-0 cell — the ruling's core finding does not rest on the registered knife-edge

expected_landing_disclosed: REJECT on all three conjuncts (the sim re-derives from scratch and must not trust these): R1 window at f = 0 is {49} ∌ 59, σ_adv = +9 and σ_ach = −10 simultaneously; R2 f* = {10, 15, 19} with 68 → 19 = s margin-0 and 10 > 9.5 at ratio 20/19; R3 f = 10 → σ_ach = 0 exactly, f(d = 9) = 19 = s. Reporting sharpenings: seller/buyer fork at f = 0 is +$9.51 vs −$10; the only f-invariant saving is the seller's $0.80 second fee; the sibling's committed $3 floor leaves a $7 premium (the repair needs a NEW committed value)

falsifiability_worlds.f_ge_10_cells: every f ≥ 10 grid cell lands the APPROVE clauses — a committed floor ≥ 10 anywhere in the packet flips the ruling to coherent-as-shipped (re-run with the measured f)
falsifiability_worlds.basis_reading: on the vs-suggested reading the committed copy is TRUE as stated — the pre-registered NULL axis ships the gap table, not a scolding
falsifiability_worlds.t1_perturbation: the T1 independence claim dies on any world where the gap moves with a or p (checked on the perturbed controls)

conventions.C1: all decision arithmetic exact: Arm A Fractions/ints, Arm B integer cents (paths) and integer milli-dollars (fee layer); no floats anywhere in a decision path
conventions.C2: Arm B is INDEPENDENTLY WRITTEN: literal integer-cent enumeration — path costs by direct min() over enumerated w, w* by upward scan, window membership by scanning candidate bundle prices, the basis gap by direct subtraction, f* and the strict-discount law by upward floor scans — no closed-form reuse; the second decision evaluator reads Arm B numbers only
conventions.C3: disclosed-anchor comparison: F3 census anchors are registered GATES; the drafter disclosed NO seeded Arm-R counts, so Arm R prints beside exact expectations with no comparison; any mismatch between a re-derived value and a disclosed registration numeral raises a FIRST-CLASS ANOMALY, never a silent gate
conventions.C4: the falsifiability worlds are named registration cells (every f ≥ 10 cell; the perturbed-a/p controls); the APPROVE-liveness check confirms the branch fires there WITHOUT adding a decision path beyond the registered clause
conventions.C5: typed margin ledger (the V086 💡, applied): must-equal rows gated as two-sided equalities, must-clear rows gated strictly positive; no UNregistered decision comparison at margin 0; the R1-independence-of-the-margin-0-cell robustness note is reporting-only
conventions.C6: Arm R estimand + draw discipline pinned: one uniform per episode, cumulative-pmf mapping, ties to bundle (w ≥ w*), draw counts asserted; NO statistical gate
conventions.C7: seed constructor registry: exactly 20261660, 20261661, 20261662 constructed, in that order; aux 20261663 asserted never read; registry high-water 20261663 (reserved; highest read 20261662)
conventions.C8: print discipline: every registered quantity is a small integer or small Fraction; gates compare in memory FIRST and print second
conventions.C9: canonical serialization: sorted keys, Fractions as 'p/q' strings, no timestamps in results.json or stdout (byte-identity across process runs)
conventions.C10: the T3 affine-family check runs at every (α, β) grid pair; decision clauses read ONLY the committed (871/1000, 4/5) pair
conventions.C11: floor-granularity control: thresholds re-scanned at quantum 1 cent and 99 cents (a '$0.99 platform minimum' style quantum) — the integer-dollar thresholds f* = {10, 15, 19} and f(d) = 10 + d must not flip (pre-checked non-flipping, an F5 control)
conventions.C12: APPROVE/R1 mutual exclusivity verified as arithmetic on the full grid (no cell satisfies both), disclosed here before the runner
conventions.C13: exit contract: exit 0 iff all self-checks pass and exactly one ruling token issues; anomalies do not affect the exit code
